pattern: A longest-common-subsequence style dynamic program weighted by character ASCII values finds the cheapest set of deletions that makes two strings equal.
hints: Whatever survives in both strings must be a common subsequence; everything else is deleted.
Instead of maximizing matched length, minimize the ASCII cost of the characters you remove.
Build a table where dp[i][j] is the minimum delete sum for the first i and j characters: equal characters carry the diagonal cost, otherwise take the cheaper of deleting from either side.

edge_inputs.description: one string is a subsequence of the other

stress.description: random lowercase strings of moderate length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(1, min(120, size))
    m = max(1, min(120, size // 2 + 1))
    s1 = ''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(n))
    s2 = ''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(m))
    return [s1, s2]
